Analysis.description: A self-similar analysis specification. Every level has the same structure: metadata, inputs, outputs, decisions, insights, and optional sub-analyses. A sub-analysis extracted to its own file is a valid Analysis on its own.

container.description: Default container for recipes in this node. Image names are pulled; file paths are built from source.

decisions.description: Decision points in this analysis (keyed by decision ID)

findings.description: Findings and conclusions from outputs (keyed by insight ID)

id.description: Analysis identifier (used as key when nested as a sub-analysis)

narrative.description: Structured prose describing this analysis, split into five sections (summary, findings, methods, inputs, outputs). See the Narrative class for section semantics and the tree-path anchor grammar used for internal cross-references.

path.description: Path to a directory containing its own astra.yaml. Mutually exclusive with inline content fields (inputs, outputs, decisions, etc.).

prior_insights.description: Prior insights that inform decisions (keyed by insight ID)

Analysis.title: Analysis

Analysis__identifier_optional.description: A self-similar analysis specification. Every level has the same structure: metadata, inputs, outputs, decisions, insights, and optional sub-analyses. A sub-analysis extracted to its own file is a valid Analysis on its own.

container.description: Default container for recipes in this node. Image names are pulled; file paths are built from source.

decisions.description: Decision points in this analysis (keyed by decision ID)

findings.description: Findings and conclusions from outputs (keyed by insight ID)

id.description: Analysis identifier (used as key when nested as a sub-analysis)

narrative.description: Structured prose describing this analysis, split into five sections (summary, findings, methods, inputs, outputs). See the Narrative class for section semantics and the tree-path anchor grammar used for internal cross-references.

path.description: Path to a directory containing its own astra.yaml. Mutually exclusive with inline content fields (inputs, outputs, decisions, etc.).

prior_insights.description: Prior insights that inform decisions (keyed by insight ID)

Analysis__identifier_optional.title: Analysis

Decision.description: A decision point in the analysis. Can be locally defined (with label, options) or a reference to a parent decision via 'from'.

default.description: Default option ID for baseline universes

from.description: Reference to a parent decision using '../' prefix (e.g., '../z_min'). When set, this is a pure reference and must not have label, options, or default.

id.description: Decision identifier (the key in the decisions map)

options.description: Map of option IDs to option specifications

when.description: Conditions for when this element is active. Format: 'decision_id.option_id' or '~decision_id.option_id'. Multiple conditions are AND'd together.

Decision.title: Decision

DecisionSelection.description: A mapping from a decision ID to the selected option ID
DecisionSelection.title: DecisionSelection

DecisionSelection__identifier_optional.description: A mapping from a decision ID to the selected option ID
DecisionSelection__identifier_optional.title: DecisionSelection

Decision__identifier_optional.description: A decision point in the analysis. Can be locally defined (with label, options) or a reference to a parent decision via 'from'.

default.description: Default option ID for baseline universes

from.description: Reference to a parent decision using '../' prefix (e.g., '../z_min'). When set, this is a pure reference and must not have label, options, or default.

id.description: Decision identifier (the key in the decisions map)

options.description: Map of option IDs to option specifications

when.description: Conditions for when this element is active. Format: 'decision_id.option_id' or '~decision_id.option_id'. Multiple conditions are AND'd together.

Decision__identifier_optional.title: Decision

Evidence.description: Evidence from a source with W3C-compliant selectors. Can reference literature (by DOI) or analysis artifacts (by output ID). Exactly one of doi or artifact must be set.

artifact.description: Output ID referencing a declared output in this analysis

snapshot.description: Path to immutable copy of the artifact

source_commit.description: Git commit that produced the original artifact

version.description: Paper version for arXiv papers (version matters for reproducibility)

Evidence.title: Evidence

FragmentSelector.description: W3C FragmentSelector for PDF locations. Conforms to RFC 3778/8118 for PDF fragments.
FragmentSelector.title: FragmentSelector

Input.description: An input to the analysis. Two kinds: data (dataset/file/resource) or analysis (outputs from another ASTRA analysis). Sub-analysis inputs can use 'from' to reference a parent input or a sibling's output (e.g., 'sibling_id.output_id').

from.description: Reference to parent input or sibling output (e.g., 'input_id' or 'sibling.output_id').

label.description: Short human-readable name for compact rendering (margin glyphs, breadcrumbs, card titles). Optional; tooling falls back to id when absent.

source.description: URI or path to the data source

use_outputs.description: Specific outputs to use from referenced analysis

Input.title: Input

Insight.description: A unit of scientific knowledge backed by evidence. Used for both prior_insights (informing decisions) and findings (conclusions from the analysis).

derived.description: True if synthesized/inferred from multiple sources

label.description: Short human-readable name for compact rendering (margin glyphs, breadcrumbs, card titles). Optional; tooling falls back to id when absent.

Insight.title: Insight

InsightCollection.description: Collection of insights, usable standalone or embedded in an analysis

insights.description: Map of insight IDs to insights

InsightCollection.title: InsightCollection

Insight__identifier_optional.description: A unit of scientific knowledge backed by evidence. Used for both prior_insights (informing decisions) and findings (conclusions from the analysis).

derived.description: True if synthesized/inferred from multiple sources

label.description: Short human-readable name for compact rendering (margin glyphs, breadcrumbs, card titles). Optional; tooling falls back to id when absent.

Insight__identifier_optional.title: Insight

Narrative.description:
Structured prose describing an analysis, organized into five sections: summary, findings, methods, inputs, and outputs. All sections are schema-optional, but ``astra validate`` applies a conditional requirement: a section must hold non-empty prose when the corresponding structured data exists on the Analysis node.
- ``findings`` required when Analysis.findings has entries. - ``methods`` required when Analysis.decisions or
  Analysis.analyses has entries.
- ``inputs`` required when Analysis.inputs has entries. - ``outputs`` required when Analysis.outputs has entries. - ``summary`` is always optional — no structured counterpart.
Authors narrate what they declare; stub analyses with only a summary stay clean.
Section content is Markdown. Internal references to other elements of the analysis use anchor links of the form ``[text](#path.to.element)``. References may appear in any section — coverage is resolved across the whole narrative, not per-section — so an author is free to cite a finding from the summary, or an input from the methods section.
Anchor grammar is tree-path-first, matching the rest of ASTRA's reference syntax (e.g. 'sibling.output_id' in 'from'). Sub-analyses are traversed before the category:

  [scaling decision](#decisions.scaling)
  [scaling option](#decisions.scaling.options.standard)
  [finding](#findings.best_model)
  [prior insight](#prior_insights.compute_scaling)
  [input](#inputs.iris_data)
  [sub-analysis output](#preprocessing.outputs.features)
  [sub-analysis decision](#preprocessing.decisions.scaling)
  [sub-analysis](#analyses.preprocessing)

References are interpreted relative to the hosting analysis. Use '../' prefix to escape to parent scope, as with decision 'from' (e.g. [see parent](#../decisions.method)).

findings.description: Narrative discussion of the analysis's findings. Individual findings live under Analysis.findings as structured Insight objects; this section is the prose that frames them.

inputs.description: Narrative discussion of the analysis's inputs. Individual inputs live under Analysis.inputs as structured objects; this section frames them.

methods.description: Narrative discussion of the methodology, including decision points and any sub-analyses. Structured decisions and nested analyses live under Analysis.decisions and Analysis.analyses; this section frames them.

outputs.description: Narrative discussion of the expected outputs. Individual outputs live under Analysis.outputs as structured objects; this section frames them.

summary.description: High-level overview of the analysis — its question, scope, and a brief orientation for readers.

Narrative.title: Narrative

Option.description: An option for a decision point

excluded.description: Whether this option was considered and rejected

id.description: Option identifier (the key in the options map)

incompatible_with.description: Decision.option pairs that cannot be selected together

requires.description: Decision.option pairs that must also be selected

Option.title: Option

Option__identifier_optional.description: An option for a decision point

excluded.description: Whether this option was considered and rejected

id.description: Option identifier (the key in the options map)

incompatible_with.description: Decision.option pairs that cannot be selected together

requires.description: Decision.option pairs that must also be selected

Option__identifier_optional.title: Option

Output.description: An expected output from the analysis. Outputs can declare their provenance via 'from' to trace which sub-analysis produces them.

decisions.description:
Decision IDs (in the surrounding scope) that parameterize this output. Declares the output's provenance contract: re-running with a different option for any listed decision must be expected to produce a different output.
Runners use this to (a) compute the per-output cache key, (b) determine the minimal universe set needed to materialize the output, and (c) deliver the active option values to the recipe (via flags, env vars, or a sidecar — runner's choice).
References use plain decision IDs and resolve through any `from:` chain in the surrounding analysis scope.

label.description: Short human-readable name for compact rendering (margin glyphs, breadcrumbs, card titles). Optional; tooling falls back to id when absent.

when.description: Conditions for when this element is active. Format: 'decision_id.option_id' or '~decision_id.option_id'. Multiple conditions are AND'd together.

Output.title: Output

Recipe.description:
A build rule that produces an output. Recipes follow Snakemake's rule grammar: `shell` or `script` defines the work, `input` lists upstream output IDs, `params` carries static parameters, and `threads` / `resources` / `container` / `conda` / `log` describe the execution context.
Decision-driven parameterization is declared on the parent Output via `decisions:`, not here. The recipe describes how to build the output; the output declares what it depends on.

conda.description: Path to a Conda environment YAML file. Provides an alternative to `container` for environment specification.

container.description: Container image name or path to a Containerfile. Image names (e.g., 'python:3.9', 'ghcr.io/org/img:latest') are pulled as pre-built images; file paths (e.g., 'Containerfile', 'containers/Dockerfile') are built from source.

input.description: Output IDs that must be materialized before this recipe runs. References resolve to the upstream Output node; runners are responsible for translating IDs into concrete paths.

log.description: Path (or path template) where the runner should write the recipe's stdout/stderr.

params.description: Static parameters made available to the recipe body. Values are strings; runners decide how to surface them (Snakemake `{params.x}` substitution, env vars, etc.).

script.description: Path to a script file to execute. Mutually exclusive with `shell`.

shell.description: Shell command to execute (e.g., 'python src/train.py'). Mutually exclusive with `script`.

Recipe.title: Recipe

Resources.description: Compute resource requirements for a recipe. Field names follow Snakemake's `resources:` conventions so that cluster executors (SLURM, Kubernetes, etc.) can consume them without remapping.
Resources.title: Resources

TextQuoteSelector.description: W3C TextQuoteSelector for locating text in a document. The authoritative anchor for verification.
TextQuoteSelector.title: TextQuoteSelector

Universe.description: A universe specification - a complete set of decisions across the entire analysis tree.
Universe.title: Universe

UniverseNode.description: A universe node mirroring the analysis tree structure. Represents decision selections at a specific sub-analysis node.

universe.description: Name of a universe in the sub-analysis's universes/ directory. Alternative to inline decisions.

UniverseNode.title: UniverseNode

UniverseNode__identifier_optional.description: A universe node mirroring the analysis tree structure. Represents decision selections at a specific sub-analysis node.

universe.description: Name of a universe in the sub-analysis's universes/ directory. Alternative to inline decisions.

UniverseNode__identifier_optional.title: UniverseNode
